title: eryngium prostratum
intro: Eryngium prostratum, also known as creeping eryngo, is a type of flowering plant in the parsley family native to Australia, known for its unique, blue-green flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Eryngium prostratum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and ease respiratory problems. Eryngium prostratum is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
